name: Arnold Schoenberg
abstract: Arnold Schoenberg or Schönberg (13 September 1874 – 13 July 1951) was an Austrian and American modernist composer, music theorist, and teacher associated with developing variation, the emancipation of the dissonance, and twelve-tone composition. He taught composition in Vienna and at the Prussian Academy of Arts (1925–1933), resigning in…

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: And so my composition teacher was from Vienna. He had been a friend of Arnold Schoenberg and people like that. And I knew nothing about classical music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Longineu Parsons
about_quotes.evidence: But at the same time why do I need to be restricted to it? Because what if I want to employ say some of the twelve-tone work of Arnold Schoenberg that has spent plenty of time studying and learning how to do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Longineu Parsons interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 17, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Shorter
about_quotes.evidence: Because over at USC, they were housing the Arnold Schoenberg Building, and I see Arnold Schoenberg’s name there
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Shorter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: when Mr. Schoenberg started teaching at UCLA... I’d like really to take a score and show it to him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: His music is sort of like — it makes Arnold Schoenberg look like “Tea for Two” in a sense.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: I studied with some other very great musicians, I will say Arnold Schoenberg
